name: nika-lang
description: Intent as Code — Nika, the workflow language for AI (.nika.yaml). A content-first DAG canvas that runs, replays and AUDITS your workflow before a token is spent (cost ceiling · secret-flow · permits), check-as-you-type diagnostics, schema-derived completions, agent tools. For VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, VSCodium.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: In Restricted Mode the workspace cannot override which binary the extension executes; static language features stay on.

virtualWorkspaces.description: Diagnostics, graph and runs need a real file system and the nika binary; syntax, snippets and expression intel work everywhere.

commands.title: Nika: Resume Last Run (re-run what changed)
Nika: Dry-Run (show the plan · zero effects)
Nika: Preflight — cost · secrets · permits (before any token)
Nika: Export DAG as Graphviz Dot
Nika: Diff Two Runs on the DAG
Nika: Debug This Run (Replay · Time Travel)
Nika: Export Run to OpenTelemetry (Jaeger · Grafana · Langfuse)
Nika: Fork From Task (upstream rehydrates from the trace)
Nika: Run Report (provable — read from the trace)
Nika: Run History (cross-run grid · flaky · trends)
Nika: Capture Lint Baseline (grandfather current findings)
Nika: Copy AI Authoring Prompt (deterministic protocol)
Nika: Golden Test — Update the Golden

nika.server.path.description: Path to the nika binary. Uses PATH by default.

nika.server.autoDownload.description: Automatically download the nika binary from GitHub releases if not found in PATH.

nika.server.extraArgs.description: Extra arguments passed to the nika lsp command.

nika.inlayHints.enabled.description: Show inlay hints (timeout annotations, model costs, binding labels).

nika.codeLens.enabled.description: Show code lens actions (Run, Validate, task count, last run status).

nika.trace.server.description: Trace communication between VS Code and the Nika language server.

nika.intel.enabled.description: Client-side template intelligence inside ${{ ... }} islands (completions, hover, go-to-definition for tasks/with/env/secrets/vars refs).

nika.diagnostics.runOn.description: When to run `nika check --json` for editor diagnostics (client path, used until the LSP ships). 'type' is debounced.

nika.diagnostics.workspace.markdownDescription: Lint CLOSED `*.nika.yaml` files across the workspace (`nika check --json`) so every workflow shows in the Problems panel. Capped at 300 files per sweep (logged when hit); open files stay with the live controller.

nika.diagnostics.severity.markdownDescription: Remap finding severity per code — exact (`NIKA-DAG-003`), glob family (`NIKA-SEC-*`), or client lints (`nika.literal-secret` · `nika.redundant-dep` · `nika.unused-schema`). Exact keys win over globs; `off` hides the squiggle (quick fixes stay available).

nika.secretsLint.enabled.description: Warn on literal credentials pasted in workflow YAML (pure local scan) with a ${{ env.VAR }} quick fix.

nika.traces.keep.description: Journal housekeeping: keep the newest N run journals per workflow directory (0 = unlimited). The newest always survive.

nika.traces.glob.description: Where the Runs view looks for flight-recorder traces.

nika.traces.live.description: Live-overlay a growing trace onto the open DAG panel while an engine writes a run (statuses update in real time).

nika.run.liveDag.description: Run workflows by streaming `nika run --json` into the DAG panel live (statuses paint in real time). Off = run in a terminal for raw output.

nika.replay.speed.description: Trace replay time compression in the DAG webview (6 = 6× faster than recorded, engine default).

enumDescriptions: The Nika design language — warm engineered-black canvas, one blue accent, verb-hue tiles, Martian Mono (the brand skin, pinned dark)
Adaptive — derives every surface AND the accent from your editor theme (--vscode-* variables, focusBorder as accent)
Auto — the brand skin on dark themes, the adaptive skin on light themes
nika.dag.theme.description: Visual skin of the DAG panel. High-contrast themes and forced colors always win over both.

nika.dag.followActiveEditor.description: The open DAG panel re-targets when you switch to another .nika.yaml (debounced).

nika.editor.xray.description: Ghost values: inlay hints show what each ${{ tasks.x… }} ref resolved to in the last matching recorded run.

nika.editor.runHighlight.description: Light the YAML spans of RUNNING tasks while a run or replay is at them — the source is the timeline.

nika.dag.cursorSync.description: Soft halo in the DAG on the task your editor caret is in (same workflow · panel visible).

nika.decorations.verbDots.description: Verb-tinted gutter dots on each task line — the nika.sh verb canon (infer blue · exec orange · invoke cyan · agent violet).

nika.editor.runDecorations.description: End-of-line run badges per task after a run (✓ duration · cost — failed in red): the run lives in the editor.

nika.ai.toolsEnabled.description: Register nika_check / nika_explain / nika_graph as Language Model Tools so in-editor AI agents validate workflows through the real oracle.

languageModelTools.name: nika_check
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Statically validate a Nika workflow (.nika.yaml) BEFORE running it. Returns the full machine-readable pre-flight report: conformance violations, secret leaks/egresses, permits (capability) escapes with machine-applicable fixes, schema findings, unknown tools, cost ceiling. You MUST call this after EVERY create or edit of a .nika.yaml file, and fix every finding before declaring the task done — never report success while findings remain. Not for generic YAML or GitHub Actions files.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the .nika.yaml file (defaults to the active editor).

languageModelTools.name: nika_explain
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Explain a NIKA-XXXX error code: cause, category, and the fix form. Use when a check report or diagnostic carries a code you do not recognize.

code.description: The error code, e.g. NIKA-440 (bare 440 also accepted).

languageModelTools.name: nika_graph
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Project the canonical execution DAG of a Nika workflow (graph_format 1 JSON): topologically sorted task nodes with verb, model, when-gates, fan-out and static cost intervals, plus depends_on edges. Requires the workflow to pass conformance first.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the .nika.yaml file (defaults to the active editor).

command.description: The nika subcommand to run (check · run · inspect)

walkthroughs.title: Get started with Nika
walkthroughs.description: From zero to a running AI workflow in five steps.

steps.title: Install the nika engine
steps.description:
The extension drives the `nika` binary (LSP · check · run · DAG).
[Install nika](https://nika.sh/install)
Or let the extension download it · it will offer automatically.

steps.title: Create your first workflow
steps.description:
Scaffold a `.nika.yaml` with the canonical envelope.
[New Workflow](command:nika.newWorkflow)

steps.title: Validate as you type
steps.description:
Diagnostics · completions · hover docs are live via the LSP. Run an explicit check ·
[Validate Workflow](command:nika.checkWorkflow)

steps.title: Run it (no API key needed)
steps.description:
The starter uses `model: mock/echo` — deterministic · zero keys.
[Run Workflow](command:nika.runWorkflow)

steps.title: See the DAG
steps.description:
Visualize task dependencies · click a node to jump to its YAML.
[Show DAG](command:nika.showDag)

steps.title: Time-travel a recorded run (F5)
steps.description:
Set a breakpoint on any task line and press **F5** — the engine replays your latest recorded run under the debugger: step forward AND backward through settles, outputs in the Variables pane. Replay never re-executes.
[Debug the latest run](command:workbench.action.debug.start)

steps.title: Make your AI an expert
steps.description:
`nika init` writes schema wiring, AGENTS.md, and Cursor rules; this command wires MCP so agents can check workflows and explain failures through the real oracle.
[Setup agents](command:nika.setupMcp)

viewsWelcome.contents:
No workflows in this folder yet.
[＋ New workflow](command:nika.newWorkflow)
[✨ Describe → generate](command:nika.generateWorkflow)
[▤ Browse embedded examples](command:nika.browseExamples)
Everything else lives under ⇧⌘P → “Nika”, or [open the menu](command:nika.showMenu).

workflow.description: The .nika.yaml the run executed (stack frames point here).

replay.description: A recorded run journal (.nika/traces/*.ndjson) — replay never re-executes.

configurationSnippets.label: Nika: Replay a recorded run
configurationSnippets.description: Time-travel debug over a .nika/traces journal (stepBack included).

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run clean && npm run compile
scripts.typecheck: tsc -p ./ && tsc --noEmit --strict --target es2020 --module es2022 --moduleResolution bundler --lib es2022,dom,dom.iterable src/webview/dag.ts
scripts.test: vitest run && node scripts/parity.mjs && npm run lint
scripts.test:integration: npm run compile && npm run compile:integration && node out-integration/runTests.js
